title: External asset manifest (external_asset_v2)
description: What an external producer delivers alongside a 3D model so the game_iso_v1 pipeline can read it. See dist/model_producer_delivery_spec_v2/. v2 makes texture_mode REQUIRED.

variant_id.description: unique per variant; becomes the output dir name, so two assets must not share it

archetype.description: logical family; selects the shared rig + animation library. Add a value here when a new archetype is introduced. (quadruped: 4-legged creatures, forelegs->arms / hindlegs->legs region reuse; ball: rolling-ball prop; dragon: winged quadruped, wings+forelegs->arms / hindlegs->legs / tail->torso / horns+jaw->head.)

mesh.description: .glb (preferred) or .obj, relative to this manifest. For a rigged+animated variant this is the glb AFTER bake_anim_from_json.py has embedded the clips, OR pair a clip-less rigged glb with files.animation_clips below.

animation_clips.description: optional path to an anim_clips_v1 JSON (schema/animation_clips.schema.json). When present, bake_asset.py runs bake_anim_from_json.py to embed these clips into files.mesh (a rigged glb) before baking.

up.description: source up axis. HONORED for .obj (a Y-up obj without up:"y" bakes sideways). For .glb/.gltf the importer reads the file's own axis and this field is currently inert -- set it correctly anyway for portability.

forward.description: The model's forward / travel heading (the way it faces). Optional -- omit it and `+x` (= direction 0) is assumed. The baker ROTATES the declared forward onto +X (a +y-authored model bakes identically to a +x-authored one), so this is a LIVE correction, not just a label. Planar only: forward is a ground-plane heading (+z/-z are not valid).

rig.description: rig profile id (pipeline/schema/rig_profiles/<rig>.json). Omit for a static mesh -> procedural animation.

default_state.description: the state shown when nothing is playing (must be an `animations` key). Omit -> engine uses `idle` if present, else the lexicographically-first state.

region_source.description: how HIT regions are tagged. Only material_name bakes today; the others are documented extensions.

textures.description: named texture maps relative to this manifest. RENDER STATUS this iteration: only an embedded-glb base color renders (Workbench TEXTURE pass); base_color listed here, and normal/roughness/metallic, are RECORDED but NOT rendered. OBJ deliveries render a flat per-region color (no texture).

clip.description: glTF animation clip name embedded in the glb (defaults to the state key)

frames.description: sprite frames sampled per direction (the same value as the matching anim_clips_v1 clip's `frames`)

playback.description: loop wraps; once holds the last frame (engine ADR-044)

animations.description: state name -> clip sampling. The pipeline samples each named glb clip into `frames` poses x direction_count directions.

world_metrics.description: optional override; otherwise measured from the mesh AABB

texture_mode.description: REQUIRED. 'textured' obliges a real UV unwrap + a baseColorTexture bound IN the glb -- verified by glb_texture_probe.texture_capable BEFORE any bake (ADR-0026). 'flat_region' shades by per-region material base colour and needs no UVs/atlas (a first-class, fully-supported delivery).

provenance.description: recording-only provenance. The texture sub-block (has_bound_tex/real_albedo/basecolor_sha256/uv_coverage_ratio/degenerate_uv_materials/uv_repaired/flat_fallback/calibration_texture/waiver_id) follows ADR-0029 / review snippet 06.

waivers.description: named, single-check, expiring downgrades (review snippet 07). A waiver must never set real_albedo true and must carry expires_at.

calibration.description: marks a debug/oracle package (review snippet 09): CALIB_RGB colour pass, debug_region_legend/skin_binding/texture_regions sidecars, real_albedo false.

radial_symmetric.description: true for radial props (ball/orb) that have no inherent front; pairs with front_back_distinctness_exempt (ADR-0033 creature traits).

front_back_distinctness_exempt.description: exempt from the front!=back distinctness gate (radial/symmetric props only).
